title: OpenAI reports AI-assisted review helped rare-disease specialists find 18 diagnoses
friendlyTitle: AI helped doctors revisit hard rare-disease cases
summary: OpenAI and Boston Children’s Hospital described an NEJM AI study where an OpenAI reasoning model reanalyzed 376 previously unsolved rare-disease cases. The AI produced evidence-linked hypotheses for specialists, contributing to 18 confirmed diagnoses after expert review and laboratory confirmation.
friendlySummary: Researchers used an OpenAI model to review old unsolved rare-disease cases. Doctors then checked the ideas, and 18 diagnoses were confirmed through proper medical testing.
whyItMatters: This is a careful example of AI improving a high-friction human workflow without replacing clinicians. For families with unresolved rare diseases, periodic AI-assisted reanalysis could help surface leads that were missed when evidence was scattered or still emerging.
friendlyWhyItMatters: This shows AI can help specialists find clues faster, while doctors still make and confirm the medical decisions.
